commit.message:
Fix Cairo assertion crash in GTK4 SKDrawingArea (#3562)

Add pix.Flush() before pix.MarkDirty() to properly release any
existing Cairo snapshot references before modifying pixel data.

Without this call, Cairo's internal snapshot mechanism can trigger
an assertion failure (or corrupt rendering) because MarkDirty()
invalidates the surface while a snapshot still holds a reference
to the underlying data. The Flush() call tells Cairo to finish
any pending reads from prior snapshots, making it safe to then
mark the surface as modified.

This matches the documented Cairo usage pattern:
  cairo_surface_flush()   — complete pending reads
  <modify pixel data>     — write via Skia
  cairo_surface_mark_dirty() — notify Cairo of changes

The crash manifests as a SIGABRT or assertion on repeated redraws
when Cairo internally snapshots the ImageSurface (e.g. during
SetSourceSurface/Paint cycles).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix tvOS NativeReference targets to split device/simulator frameworks (#3563)

The tvOS ItemGroup in IncludeNativeAssets targets was using a single
NativeReference pointing to the device framework for all tvOS builds.
This caused linker failures when building for the tvOS Simulator
because the device framework contains arm64 binaries compiled for
tvOS, not the x86_64/arm64 binaries compiled for tvOS Simulator.

Split the tvOS NativeReference to match the existing iOS pattern:
- tvossimulator RID → tvossimulator/ framework
- tvos device RID → tvos/ framework
- Guard on RuntimeIdentifier != '' to avoid evaluation during
  outer builds where RID is not yet resolved

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: swap mssql/server for azure-sql-edge on ARM64 hosts

The Azure Service Bus emulator depends on SQL Server, which has no ARM64
image. On Apple Silicon (and other ARM64 hosts), the mssql/server:2022-latest
container fails under QEMU with jemalloc address mapping errors.

Use Aspire's BeforeStartEvent to intercept the container model and swap the
image to azure-sql-edge:latest, which has native ARM64 support. The Service
Bus emulator works with either SQL engine.

Fix memory leaks in TranscriptBuilder, TranscriptTextContent, and MainWindow

TranscriptBuilder: Track all PropertyChanged handlers subscribed on
ChatMessageViewModel in _pendingToolHandlers list. Unsubscribe all pending
handlers in ResetState() (called on every chat switch via Rebuild). Handlers
that complete naturally (tool status → Completed/Failed) also remove
themselves from the tracking list. Fixes 5 handler sites (todo, terminal,
generic tool, subagent, assistant streaming) that previously only
unsubscribed on completion — abandoned/in-progress tools leaked forever.

TranscriptTextContent: Add OnDetachedFromVisualTree to clear StrataMarkdown
content and null the Content property, allowing the child StrataMarkdown to
release its caches.

MainWindow: Track project filter bar PropertyChanged handlers and unsubscribe
all of them at the start of RebuildProjectFilterBar before subscribing new
ones. Add guard (_wiredVm) to prevent double-wiring event handlers if
OnDataContextChanged fires multiple times for the same ViewModel.
